title: Boat Rental in Sardinia 2026: A Day on the Water
html:
Planning a day on the water in Sardinia means choosing from dozens of departure points around the island, from the Costa Smeralda to Cagliari. Typical half-day rentals start around EUR 300 for a small motorboat, while a crewed yacht for a full day can exceed EUR 1,500. BoatHire24 connects you with skippers, bareboats and luxury charters across the island, making it easy to compare options.

Where to set off in Sardinia

Your departure point shapes the whole day. The most popular marinas are Porto Cervo on the north-east coast, Porto Rotondo nearby, and Olbia's Isola Bianca terminal for ferries and charters. On the south coast, Cagliari's Marina Piccola offers access to the Poetto beaches and the Devil's Saddle. On the west, Alghero's port leads to the Neptune's Grotto, while the Maddalena Archipelago is best accessed from Palau or La Maddalena island. Each harbour has its own character and fleet. You can day charters in Sardinia for your chosen dates.

Which boat to rent

Your choice depends on group size, budget and whether you want to drive or relax.

  1. Small motorboat (4-6 guests), perfect for exploring coves like Cala Luna; from EUR 350 half-day.
  2. Sailing yacht (6-8 guests), ideal for a quieter day among the Maddalena islands; from EUR 600 full-day.
  3. Catamaran (8-12 guests), stable and spacious for families or groups; from EUR 1,200 full-day.
  4. Crewed motor yacht (10-14 guests), luxury on the water, like the Princess F70 or Azimut 60; from EUR 2,500 full-day. For full specs and booking, visit the Princess F70 charter page.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Sardinia?

Prices range from EUR 150 per person for a shared tour to over EUR 5,000 for a superyacht day charter. One option worth a look is the Princess F70 charter page.

  • Shared RIB tour: EUR 80-150 per person, 3-4 hours, up to 12 guests.
  • Small motorboat half-day (self-drive): EUR 300-500, 4-6 guests, no skipper.
  • Catamaran full-day (skipper included): EUR 1,200-2,500, 8-12 guests.
  • Crewed luxury yacht full-day (skipper, host, fuel): EUR 2,500-5,000, 10-14 guests.

These are ballpark figures for the 2026 high season. Booking early often secures better rates. You can compare verified listings and book day charters in Sardinia direct on BoatHire24.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Sardinia?

For motorboats over 40hp you need a licence, but many operators offer licence-free rentals with a skipper. You can also see more on the BoatHire24 marketplace.

If you plan to explore the Maddalena Archipelago, start early to avoid the midday crowds and the Ponente wind that picks up from the west by afternoon.

In Italy, a valid boating licence (patente nautica) is required for motorboats above 30-40hp (depending on distance from shore) and for any vessel over 10 metres. However, most rental companies provide a skipper for EUR 150-250 per day, so you can relax without a licence. Always confirm exact requirements with the operator when you book.

At a glance

  • Price range: EUR 150-5,000 per day
  • Peak months: July, August, early September
  • Trip length: half-day (4h) or full-day (8h)
  • Guest capacity: 4 to 14 typically
  • Currency: EUR (Euro)

Questions about boat rental in Sardinia

Can I rent a boat without a licence in Sardinia?

Yes, by booking a boat with a skipper, you can enjoy the day without holding a licence and the skipper handles navigation.

What is the best month for a boat trip in Sardinia?

June and September are ideal because the weather is warm, the sea calm, and the anchorages less crowded than July and August.

How far is the Maddalena Archipelago from Palau?

The distance from Palau to the main island of La Maddalena is about 2 nautical miles, a short 15-minute crossing.

Is it possible to visit the Gulf of Orosei by boat?

Yes, the Gulf of Orosei with its famous coves like Cala Goloritzé and Cala Mariolu is best explored from Cala Gonone harbour.

Whether you want to skipper your own boat or sit back on a crewed charter, Sardinia offers some of the Mediterranean’s most stunning coastal scenery. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Sardinia or list your own. If you own a boat in Sardinia, consider listing it on the BoatHire24 marketplace to reach thousands of travellers exploring the island in 2026.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Sardinia and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
